intent.summary: Ship Rubrix v1.2.0 — Measurement-Based Lock Gates
intent.details: Replace user-fiat lock with measurement-gated lock. Add clarity-scorer agent (4-axis weighted scoring), .clarity schema field on rubric/matrix/plan, version-aware enforcement (only contract version >= 1.2 enforces clarity), rubrix score-clarity read-only CLI, rubrix lock --force audit path, PostToolUse deduction inline surface, and rubrix report Forced Locks section. v1.0/v1.1 contracts remain read-compatible.

criteria.description: cli/schemas/rubrix.schema.json gains $defs.Clarity (score, threshold, deductions[code,message,weight], scored_at, scorer_version, artifact_hash, forced + audit fields) referenced from rubric/matrix/plan as optional. Deduction code is enum {vague_description, missing_evidence, unmeasurable_floor, dangling_reference, uncovered_axis}. v1.0 (examples/self-eval, examples/ios-refactor) and v1.1 fixtures continue to validate clean; clarity.score>1, deductions.code outside enum, forced=true without forced_at+force_reason, and non-64-hex artifact_hash all fail validation.

cli/src/core/contract.ts exports Clarity, ClarityDeduction, ClarityDeductionCode, and ArtifactKey types; rubric/matrix/plan TypeScript interfaces gain optional clarity?: Clarity. Existing v1.0/v1.1 type assignability for fixtures (no clarity) preserved.
criteria.verify: tsc --noEmit (transitively via vitest tsx loader) + manual review

criteria.description: cli/src/core/version.ts provides parseVersion, compareVersions, isAtLeast, and isV12Plus(c). isV12Plus returns true for '1.2.0' and '1.5.0', false for '0.1.0' (current dogfood baseline) and '1.1.99'. Invalid version strings yield isV12Plus=false (no throw at the boundary).
criteria.verify: vitest cli/tests/clarity-threshold.test.ts (covers via resolveClarityThreshold) + targeted module-level tests in PR #2 if needed

criteria.description: resolveClarityThreshold(c, key, opts) returns base{rubric:0.75,matrix:0.80,plan:0.70} adjusted by max-modifier across all 5 axes (deep=+0.10, standard=0, light=-0.10). Override (CLI flag) takes precedence over axis_depth, clamped to [0,1]. RUBRIX_SKIP_BRIEF=1 and uncalibrated brief fall back to standard. ambition=demo forces all-light.

rubrix score-clarity <key> <path> emits a JSON object {artifact, artifact_hash, threshold, threshold_policy_version, scorer_version, score, deductions, scored_at, ok, scorer_active} on stdout. Exit codes: 0 success, 2 invalid key/contract, 3 artifact missing. Crucially: rubrix.json byte content is unchanged after the command runs (read-only contract). v1.0/v1.1 contracts return scorer_active=false with score=1 (read-compat).

Two consecutive `score-clarity rubric <path>` invocations on the same input emit the same artifact_hash (SHA-256 over canonical, sorted-keys JSON of the artifact body, with any pre-existing clarity stripped) and the same deductions in identical order. Threshold reflects --threshold override and axis_depth.

(PR #2) cli/src/core/clarity.ts:scoreClarity is a deterministic heuristic stub that yields the same score and ordered deductions for the same artifact body. Heuristics: short text (<60 chars) → vague_description weight 0.10; vague tokens (good/well/잘/좋은/적절/충분/...) → vague_description weight 0.05; missing verify → missing_evidence weight 0.10; rubric criterion with axis tagged deep but no floor → unmeasurable_floor weight 0.15; matrix row referencing unknown rubric id → dangling_reference weight 0.20; uncovered axis/criterion/row → uncovered_axis weight 0.20. Sorted by code (vague→missing→unmeasurable→dangling→uncovered) then message. score = max(0, 1 − Σ weights), rounded to 4 decimals. scorer_version = clarity-scorer/1.0.

(PR #2) rubrix lock <key> <path> on a contract with version >= 1.2.0 calls scoreClarity after integrity checks. On score < threshold the command exits 3 and prints `[code] message (weight)` lines on stderr without persisting clarity or transitioning state. On score >= threshold the command writes c[key].clarity = result.clarity (forced=false) before transitioning to *Locked. v1.0/v1.1 contracts (version < 1.2.0) skip the gate entirely (read-compat).

rubrix validate on a v1.2 contract (version >= 1.2.0) at state ∈ {RubricLocked, MatrixDrafted, MatrixLocked, PlanDrafted, PlanLocked, Scoring, Passed, Failed} fails with a v1.2-specific error if any locked artifact is missing clarity, or if clarity.score < clarity.threshold and clarity.forced!==true. forced=true with forced_at + force_reason validates as an audited bypass.

(codex review P2 follow-up) The v1.2 clarity invariant is enforced uniformly across every code path that trusts locks: rubrix validate, rubrix gate (refuses to evaluate when invariant is breached, exit 2), PreToolUse hook (blocks code-edits and /rubrix:score on a v1.2 contract missing clarity, while still allowing edits to rubrix.json itself so users can fix it), and UserPromptExpansion. Logic lives in cli/src/core/clarity-gate.ts (checkClarityInvariants + firstClarityViolation) and is consumed by validate.ts, gate.ts, and hooks/handlers.ts.

examples/self-eval/rubrix.json (v1.0, version='0.1.0', no brief) and examples/ios-refactor/rubrix.json (v1.0, no brief) continue to validate, gate, and report unchanged. v1.1 fixtures (version='0.1.0' + intent.brief) at PlanLocked also remain valid without clarity. The 173-test baseline is fully preserved.

cli/src/cli.ts registers `score-clarity <key> <path>` with --threshold (numeric) and --json flags via commander; non-numeric --threshold throws at parse time; the command is reachable via `node cli/bin/rubrix.js score-clarity rubric ./rubrix.json`.
criteria.verify: manual: node cli/bin/rubrix.js score-clarity rubric ./rubrix.json (exit 0 + JSON on stdout)

criteria.description: All vitest tests pass (173 baseline preserved + 44 PR #1 tests across clarity-schema, clarity-threshold, score-clarity, integration/clarity-version-gate, clarity-gate, integration/clarity-hook-gate). No skipped or pending tests introduced. CLI, schema, and AJV validator caches behave correctly under repeated invocations.

deductions.message: criterion `clarity_scorer_stub_deterministic` description uses vague language; replace generic words with measurable terms

rows.evidence_required: AJV validation of cli/schemas/rubrix.schema.json: (a) accepts $defs.Clarity references on rubric/matrix/plan; (b) rejects clarity.score>1, deduction.code outside enum, forced=true without forced_at+force_reason, artifact_hash not 64-hex; (c) v1.0/v1.1 fixtures (examples/self-eval, examples/ios-refactor, repo-root pre-v1.2) keep validating clean.

TypeScript type-check via tsx loader passes for cli/src/core/contract.ts exports (Clarity, ClarityDeduction, ClarityDeductionCode, ArtifactKey) and rubric/matrix/plan optional clarity?: Clarity. v1.0/v1.1 fixtures still type-assignable as RubrixContract without clarity field.
rows.verify: vitest (transitive type-check) + manual review of cli/src/core/contract.ts

rows.evidence_required: Direct calls to parseVersion('1.2.0'), compareVersions('1.2.0','1.1.99'), isAtLeast('1.2.0','1.2.0'), isV12Plus({version:'0.1.0'}), isV12Plus({version:'1.2.0'}), and isV12Plus({version:'not-a-version'}) all produce expected boolean / numeric results without throwing at the boundary.
rows.verify: vitest cli/tests/clarity-threshold.test.ts (covers via resolveClarityThreshold) + cli/src/core/version.ts module review

rows.evidence_required: 8 vitest assertions in clarity-threshold.test.ts cover: standard fallback (base unchanged), one-deep axis raises by +0.10, multiple-deep axes still cap at +0.10 (max not sum), all-light lowers by 0.10, override > axis_depth, override clamped to [0,1], RUBRIX_SKIP_BRIEF=1 fallback to standard, uncalibrated brief fallback to standard.

vitest score-clarity.test.ts covers: invalid key → exit 2; missing artifact → exit 3; valid v1.2 input → JSON with numeric score, scorer_active=true, scorer_version='clarity-scorer/1.0'; v1.0 contract → scorer_active=false, score=1; rubrix.json byte-equivalent before/after invocation; --threshold override visible; axis_depth.deep raises threshold to 0.85 for rubric.

Two consecutive scoreClarityCommand invocations on identical contract produce equal artifact_hash and equal ordered deductions[].code (deterministic). Independently verified via cli/tests/integration/clarity-determinism.test.ts which runs scoreClarity twice and asserts byte-equivalent JSON.

clarity-scorer.test.ts asserts: vague tokens trigger vague_description; description below 60 chars triggers vague_description; missing verify triggers missing_evidence; deep-axis criterion without floor triggers unmeasurable_floor; matrix dangling reference triggers dangling_reference; uncovered axis/criterion/row triggers uncovered_axis. Deductions are sorted by code then message; score = max(0, 1 − Σ weights), rounded to 4 decimals.

integration/lock-clarity-gate.test.ts asserts: vague v1.2 rubric → exit 3 + deductions on stderr + locks.rubric stays false + state stays RubricDrafted + clarity not persisted. Well-formed v1.2 rubric → exit 0 + locks.rubric=true + state=RubricLocked + c.rubric.clarity persisted with forced=false. v1.0 rubric (version 0.1.0) → scorer skipped, lock proceeds as before.

integration/clarity-version-gate.test.ts asserts: v1.0/v1.1 PlanLocked w/o clarity → valid; v1.2 RubricLocked w/o rubric.clarity → fail with 'v1.2 contract requires rubric.clarity'; v1.2 PlanLocked surfaces violations on every locked artifact; v1.2 score<threshold + forced=false → fail with 'lock should have refused'; v1.2 score<threshold + forced=true + force_reason → audited bypass passes.

integration/clarity-hook-gate.test.ts asserts: PreToolUse blocks Edit on v1.2 PlanLocked w/o plan.clarity; PreToolUse still allows Edit on rubrix.json itself (escape hatch); PreToolUse blocks /rubrix:score; UserPromptExpansion mirrors block on /rubrix:score; v1.0 PlanLocked w/o clarity unaffected; gateCommand on v1.2 Scoring + missing clarity → exit 2 + 'v1.2 clarity invariant'; gateCommand on v1.0 Scoring → normal evaluation.

Manual rubrix validate examples/self-eval/rubrix.json and examples/ios-refactor/rubrix.json both exit 0 (warning only on ios-refactor for missing brief; no error). 173-test baseline (pre-v1.2) all green. v1.0 fixtures untouched on disk.
rows.verify: manual `node cli/bin/rubrix.js validate examples/*/rubrix.json` + cli/tests baseline preserved

rows.evidence_required: Direct CLI invocation `node cli/bin/rubrix.js score-clarity rubric ./rubrix.json` exits 0 with JSON on stdout. `--threshold abc` (non-numeric) throws at commander parse time before scoreClarityCommand runs. `--json` flag accepted (default JSON output preserved). `score-clarity bogus ./rubrix.json` returns exit 2.

`cd cli && npm test` (vitest) reports 0 failures and 0 skipped tests. Test file count = 24+ and total tests >= 217 (PR #1 baseline). PR #2 adds clarity-scorer.test.ts and lock-clarity-gate / clarity-determinism integration tests on top. Snapshot of stdout includes 'Test Files' and 'Tests' summary lines with no 'failed' counts.

deductions.message: matrix row `row-lock-v12-gate` evidence_required uses vague language

steps.action: PR #1 (RUB-22): add $defs.Clarity to cli/schemas/rubrix.schema.json with deduction-code enum + forced/forced_at/force_reason invariant; export Clarity, ClarityDeduction, ClarityDeductionCode, ArtifactKey from cli/src/core/contract.ts; add cli/src/core/version.ts (parseVersion, compareVersions, isAtLeast, isV12Plus); add resolveClarityThreshold + axis-depth modifier table to cli/src/core/brief.ts.

PR #1 (RUB-22): add cli/src/commands/score-clarity.ts as a read-only placeholder (artifact_hash + threshold only); register `rubrix score-clarity <key> <path>` with --threshold + --json in cli/src/cli.ts; extend cli/src/commands/validate.ts to fail v1.2+ contracts at *Locked states without clarity.

PR #1 (RUB-22) codex review P2 follow-up: extract checkClarityInvariants + firstClarityViolation into cli/src/core/clarity-gate.ts; consume from cli/src/commands/validate.ts, cli/src/commands/gate.ts (refuses to evaluate when invariant breached), and cli/src/hooks/handlers.ts (PreToolUse + UserPromptExpansion block); allow editing rubrix.json itself even when invariant breached.

PR #2 (RUB-23): add agents/clarity-scorer.md (4-axis weighted spec, JSON-only, deterministic); implement scoreClarity heuristic stub in cli/src/core/clarity.ts (5 deduction codes, sorted, score = max(0, 1 − Σ weights)); wire isV12Plus + scoreClarity into cli/src/commands/lock.ts (exit 3 on score<threshold, persist clarity on success); replace score-clarity placeholder with real scorer; v1.0/v1.1 contracts skip the gate.

PR #2 (RUB-23) codex review P2 follow-ups: rewrite hashArtifact to canonicalize({key, body, context}) so axis_depth / criterion-id / row-id changes always produce a different hash; expose --threshold on the rubrix lock commander registration in cli.ts; update agents/clarity-scorer.md spec; add 2 regression tests in clarity-determinism.test.ts.

PR #3 (RUB-24): add --force <reason> to cli/src/commands/lock.ts (persists forced=true + forced_at + force_reason; emits stderr audit warning); register --force on the rubrix lock commander in cli/src/cli.ts; extend cli/src/commands/report.ts with a v1.2-only Forced Locks section (artifact / score / threshold / forced_at / reason). Empty case prints 'No forced locks.'

PR #3 (RUB-24): extend cli/src/hooks/handlers.ts:handlePostToolUse to emit <rubrix-suggestion> blocks via additionalContext for (a) the most recent rubrix lock failure (parses 'below threshold' + [code] lines from tool_response stderr) and (b) any forced lock currently persisted on the contract. v1.0/v1.1 contracts emit nothing.

PR #3 (RUB-24): walk root rubrix.json from MatrixLocked → PlanLocked (via lock plan, no --force) → Scoring → Passed by recording per-criterion scores backed by PR #28 / PR #29 evidence and running `rubrix gate ./rubrix.json --apply`. Final state=Passed with all locks=true and Forced Locks section empty.

scores.notes: 8 clarity-schema.test.ts assertions pass; v1.0 (examples/self-eval, examples/ios-refactor) and v1.1 fixtures (with brief, no clarity) keep validating clean; clarity.score>1, deductions.code outside enum, forced=true without forced_at+force_reason, and non-64-hex artifact_hash all reject as expected.

RubrixContract / Clarity / ClarityDeduction / ClarityDeductionCode / ArtifactKey are exported from cli/src/core/contract.ts and consumed across clarity.ts, clarity-gate.ts, validate.ts, lock.ts, score-clarity.ts, brief.ts, and the entire test suite without any type errors under the vitest tsx loader.

isV12Plus correctly returns false for version='0.1.0' (v1.0/v1.1 fixtures) and true for version='1.2.0' (root dogfood). compareVersions and parseVersion exercised indirectly via clarity-threshold.test.ts (8 tests) plus clarity-hook-gate.test.ts read-compat assertions.

clarity-threshold.test.ts (8 tests) covers base / +0.10 deep / max-modifier across multiple deeps / -0.10 light via ambition=demo / override > axis_depth / [0,1] clamping / RUBRIX_SKIP_BRIEF=1 fallback / uncalibrated brief fallback.

score-clarity.test.ts (8 tests) covers invalid key (exit 2), missing artifact (exit 3), v1.2 success path with numeric score and scorer_active=true, v1.0/v1.1 read-compat scorer_active=false with score=1, byte-equivalent rubrix.json before/after, --threshold override, and axis_depth.deep raising threshold to 0.85 for rubric.

After codex review P2-1 fix (commit db79fab), hashArtifact hashes canonicalize({key, body, context}). clarity-determinism.test.ts proves: byte-equivalent JSON across runs (excluding scored_at); ordered deduction codes stable; pre-existing clarity stripped during hashing; same body + different axis_depth → different hash; same matrix body + renamed criterion id → different hash.

clarity-scorer.test.ts (10 tests) verifies all 5 deduction codes fire on the right triggers, sort order is stable (vague→missing→unmeasurable→dangling→uncovered), score = max(0, 1 − Σ weights) is clamped to [0,1], and canonicalize is key-order invariant. Stub is intentional placeholder for the LLM-side clarity-scorer agent (see agents/clarity-scorer.md).

lock-clarity-gate.test.ts (4 tests) confirms vague rubric → exit 3 + deductions on stderr + rubrix.json byte-equivalent; well-formed v1.2 rubric → locks + persists clarity (forced=false); v1.0 contract → scorer skipped; --threshold override lowers the bar. Verified manually by walking the dogfood through RubricLocked → MatrixLocked → PlanLocked without forced flags.

clarity-version-gate.test.ts (7 tests) confirms v1.0/v1.1 read-compat at PlanLocked, v1.2 RubricLocked w/o clarity → fail, v1.2 PlanLocked surfaces violations across every locked artifact, score<threshold + forced=false → fail, and forced=true + force_reason audit bypass passes.

After codex review P2 fix on PR #1 (commit a924fa8), checkClarityInvariants is shared across validate.ts, gate.ts, and hooks/handlers.ts. clarity-gate.test.ts (7 tests) + clarity-hook-gate.test.ts (7 tests) prove PreToolUse blocks Edit / score on v1.2 contracts missing clarity but allows editing rubrix.json itself, UserPromptExpansion mirrors the block, gate exits 2 on a v1.2 Scoring contract missing clarity, and v1.0 contracts pass through unchanged.

Both examples/self-eval/rubrix.json and examples/ios-refactor/rubrix.json continue to pass `node cli/bin/rubrix.js validate` (only an existing v1.0 brief warning on ios-refactor, never an error). The 173-test pre-v1.2 baseline stays green; v1.0/v1.1 contracts skip every v1.2 path (scorer, validate enforcement, gate, hook block).

After codex review P2-2 fix on PR #2 (commit db79fab), `node cli/bin/rubrix.js score-clarity rubric ./rubrix.json` and `node cli/bin/rubrix.js lock --threshold 0 plan ./rubrix.json` are both reachable; non-numeric --threshold throws at commander parse time; PR #3 added --force <reason> on rubrix lock with audit-warning stderr.

`cd cli && npm test` reports 0 failed and 0 skipped tests across PR #1/#2/#3 commits. PR #1 added 44 tests (clarity-schema, clarity-threshold, score-clarity, integration/clarity-version-gate, clarity-gate, integration/clarity-hook-gate); PR #2 added 18 (clarity-scorer, integration/lock-clarity-gate, integration/clarity-determinism); PR #3 adds force-audit + report-forced + post-tool-deduction tests on top.
